title: QC_with_scQCenrich
text: vignette runs run_qc_pipeline() Seurat objects shipped package inst/extdata/.
code: library(scQCenrich) library(Seurat) std_path <- system.file("extdata","seurat_std_small.rds", package="scQCenrich") sp_path  <- system.file("extdata","seurat_sp_small.rds", package="scQCenrich") stopifnot(nzchar(std_path), file.exists(std_path)) stopifnot(nzchar(sp_path),  file.exists(sp_path)) seurat_std_small <- readRDS(std_path) seurat_sp_small  <- readRDS(sp_path)

title: QC_with_scQCenrich
code: res <- run_qc_pipeline(   obj       = seurat_std_small,   species   = "mouse",   # external splicing objects:   spliced_obj     = seurat_sp_small,   spliced_assay   = "spliced",   spliced_layer   = "counts",   unspliced_obj   = seurat_sp_small,   unspliced_assay = "unspliced",   unspliced_layer = "counts",   report_html = TRUE,   report_file = "qc_outputs/qc_report.html",   tissue = "Skin")

title: QC_with_scQCenrich
code: # Key metadata columns head( res$obj@meta.data[, c("auto_celltype","qc_status","is_doublet")], 10)  # If report_html=TRUE, a report is written to: normalizePath("qc_outputs/qc_report.html", winslash = "/")

what: Multi-Metric Quality Control for Single-Cell RNA-Seq
title: Multi-Metric Quality Control for Single-Cell RNA-Seq
text: scQCenrich advanced, annotation-aware quality control framework single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq) data. goes beyond simple static thresholds integrating multi-metric filtering—including standard Seurat metrics optional spliced/unspliced ratios droplet data. scQCenrich option automatically handles doublets, performs cell-type auto-annotation, uniquely emphasizes rescue biologically meaningful, cohesive clusters may otherwise incorrectly flagged ‘low quality’ basic filters.

title: Multi-Metric Quality Control for Single-Cell RNA-Seq
text: can install development version scQCenrich GitHub :
code: install.packages("BiocManager") remotes::install_github(   "lemonlyy755/scQCenrich",   dependencies = TRUE,   repos = BiocManager::repositories() )  vignettes: install.packages(c("knitr","rmarkdown"))      if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")  remotes::install_github(   "lemonlyy755/scQCenrich",   dependencies   = TRUE,   build_vignettes = TRUE,   repos          = BiocManager::repositories() ) browseVignettes("scQCenrich")

title: Multi-Metric Quality Control for Single-Cell RNA-Seq
text: basic usage example Note: SeuratWrappers work windows os.
code: library(scQCenrich) library(Seurat) library(SeuratWrappers)  seurat_std <- CreateSeuratObject(counts =  Read10X("your filtered count folder path")) seurat_sp <- as.Seurat(ReadVelocity(file = 'your velocyto loom file.loom'))   res <- run_qc_pipeline(   obj            = seurat_std,   species        = "human", # edit your species: mouse or human   annot_method   = "marker_score",   tissue         = c("Blood", "Immune system"), # replace with the tissue(s) matching your dataset   spliced_obj    = seurat_sp,   unspliced_obj  = seurat_sp,   report_file    = "qc_outputs/qc_report.html" )  browseURL(res$report)

title: Multi-Metric Quality Control for Single-Cell RNA-Seq
text: use annot_method = "marker_score", setting tissue correctly important specific PanglaoDB-based cell labels. PBMC-like immune data, broad missing tissue selection can blur closely related populations monocytes vs dendritic cells NK cells vs gamma delta T cells. pass unrecognised tissue name, run_qc_pipeline() warns annotation falls back tissues, less specific. Use list_panglao_tissues() see supported PanglaoDB tissue names: Examples: PBMC / whole blood: tissue = c("Blood", "Immune system") Cardiac data: tissue = "Heart"
code: list_panglao_tissues()

title: Multi-Metric Quality Control for Single-Cell RNA-Seq
text: basic example convert velocyto loom file seurat obj rds used windows OS setting: Note: step done windows.
code: library(Seurat) library(velocyto.R) library(SeuratWrappers)   ldat <- ReadVelocity(file = '/path/to/your/loomfile.loom')  # 1) Ensure unique feature names within each layer for (nm in intersect(names(ldat), c("spliced","unspliced","ambiguous"))) {   rn <- rownames(ldat[[nm]])   rn[is.na(rn) | rn == ""] <- paste0("gene_", seq_along(rn))   rownames(ldat[[nm]]) <- make.unique(rn)  # enforce uniqueness }  # 2) Keep only the shared features across layers and align their order has_layer <- intersect(names(ldat), c("spliced","unspliced","ambiguous")) features <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(ldat[has_layer], rownames)) for (nm in has_layer) {   ldat[[nm]] <- ldat[[nm]][features, , drop = FALSE] }  # 3) Ensure cell barcodes are unique and aligned across layers cells <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(ldat[has_layer], colnames)) stopifnot(length(cells) > 0) for (nm in has_layer) {   # If your loom has duplicate barcodes (rare), make them unique   cn <- colnames(ldat[[nm]])   if (anyDuplicated(cn)) colnames(ldat[[nm]]) <- make.unique(cn)   ldat[[nm]] <- ldat[[nm]][, cells, drop = FALSE] }  # 4) Convert to Seurat (v5) bm <- as.Seurat(ldat)   # should now pass without the LogMap error saveRDS(bm,"testloom.rds")

title: Multi-Metric Quality Control for Single-Cell RNA-Seq
text: scQCenrich comes bundled small toy dataset (toy_seu) can use immediately test pipeline:
code: library(scQCenrich)  # Load the built-in toy Seurat object data("toy_seu") print(toy_seu)  # Run the QC pipeline on the toy dataset res_toy <- run_qc_pipeline(   obj         = toy_seu,   species     = "human",   method      = "gmm",   qc_strength = "auto",   report_file = "qc_outputs/toy_qc_report.html" )  browseURL(res_toy$report)

title: Multi-Metric Quality Control for Single-Cell RNA-Seq
text: wish compare scQCenrich’s performance standard metrics, can write short benchmarking script. snippet highlights one might track cells retained across different methodologies: complete benchmarking script can found inst/benchmarking.r Main Features run_qc_pipeline() – one-liner wrapper full QC workflow plots reports. Vignettes See package vignettes detailed tutorials
code: library(scQCenrich) library(Seurat) library(ggplot2)  # Assuming 'seu' is your starting Seurat object.  # 1. Base Seurat thresholding seu$keep_seurat <- with(seu@meta.data,                          nFeature_RNA > 200 & nFeature_RNA < 10000 & percent.mt < 10)  # 2. scQCenrich (with just GMM auto-thresholding on basic metrics) metrics <- calcQCmetrics(obj = seu, species = "human", add_to_meta = FALSE) scQC_res <- flagLowQuality(metrics = metrics, method = "gmm", qc_strength = "auto") seu$keep_scQC <- scQC_res$qc_status != "remove"  # You can then cross-tabulate retention rates table(Seurat = seu$keep_seurat, scQC = seu$keep_scQC)  # Or visualize retention per cluster/cell_type # (Assuming your object has a 'cell_type' or 'seurat_clusters' column) retention_df <- data.frame(   CellType = seu$cell_type,   Seurat_Retained = seu$keep_seurat,   scQC_Retained = seu$keep_scQC ) # ... grouping and ggplot logic similar to standard analyses

title: Auto-annotate cell types (multi-backend wrapper) — auto_annotate
code: auto_annotate(   obj,   species = c("mouse", "human"),   assay = "RNA",   prefer = c("sctype", "marker_score", "singleR", "label_transfer"),   level = c("cluster", "cell"),   marker_score_level = c("cluster", "cell"),   custom_signatures = NULL,   ref_seurat = NULL,   ref_label_col = NULL,   unknown_min_genes = 2,   unknown_min_margin = 0.01,   unknown_min_top = 0.01,   organ = NULL,   sctype_db_path = NULL,   marker_method = c("module_score", "findmarkers"),   panglao_file = system.file("extdata", "PanglaoDB_markers_27_Mar_2020.tsv", package =     utils::packageName()),   panglao_tissue = NULL,   panglao_ui_max = 0.2,   panglao_canonical_only = TRUE,   fm_top_n = 50,   fm_padj_max = 0.05,   overlap_mode = c("count", "jaccard") )

title: Auto-annotate cell types (multi-backend wrapper) — auto_annotate
text: obj Seurat object. species Character, "mouse" "human". assay Assay use (default "RNA"). prefer Optional character; preferred backend(s) try first. level "cell" "cluster". marker_score_level Level marker_score ("cell"/"cluster"). custom_signatures Optional list/data.frame custom markers. ref_seurat Optional Seurat reference object (label transfer). ref_label_col Column ref_seurat@meta.data labels. unknown_min_genes Integer; minimum genes avoid "unknown". unknown_min_margin Numeric; min score margin call label. unknown_min_top Integer; min top markers expressed call label. organ Optional organ/tissue context string. sctype_db_path Optional path ScType flat DB (xlsx). marker_method Marker backend using marker-based annotation. panglao_file Optional PanglaoDB CSV/XLSX file path. panglao_tissue Optional tissue filter PanglaoDB. panglao_ui_max Integer; cap markers per type avoid overplot. panglao_canonical_only Logical; keep canonical markers. fm_top_n Integer; FindMarkers top N keep per cluster. fm_padj_max Numeric; padj cutoff FindMarkers filtering. overlap_mode combine/overlap multiple marker sources.

title: Auto-annotate cell types (multi-backend wrapper) — auto_annotate
text: Seurat object annotation columns added.

what: Calculate QC metrics (UMIs, genes, mito%, MALAT1, stress; optional splicing metrics) Computes nCount/nFeature, pctMT, MALAT1 fraction, stress_score (case-insensitive SYMBOL match), and optional splicing metrics from same object layers or external Seurat objects. Compatible with Seurat v5 layers and v4 slots. Returns a data.frame and (if add_to_meta=TRUE) writes *_QC fields into obj@meta.data (no regression in field names). — calcQCmetrics
title: Calculate QC metrics (UMIs, genes, mito%, MALAT1, stress; optional splicing metrics) Computes nCount/nFeature, pctMT, MALAT1 fraction, stress_score (case-insensitive SYMBOL match), and optional splicing metrics from same object layers or external Seurat objects. Compatible with Seurat v5 layers and v4 slots. Returns a data.frame and (if add_to_meta=TRUE) writes *_QC fields into obj@meta.data (no regression in field names). — calcQCmetrics
text: Calculate QC metrics (UMIs, genes, mito%, MALAT1, stress; optional splicing metrics) Computes nCount/nFeature, pctMT, MALAT1 fraction, stress_score (case-insensitive SYMBOL match), optional splicing metrics object layers external Seurat objects. Compatible Seurat v5 layers v4 slots. Returns data.frame (add_to_meta=TRUE) writes *_QC fields obj@meta.data (regression field names).

title: Calculate QC metrics (UMIs, genes, mito%, MALAT1, stress; optional splicing metrics) Computes nCount/nFeature, pctMT, MALAT1 fraction, stress_score (case-insensitive SYMBOL match), and optional splicing metrics from same object layers or external Seurat objects. Compatible with Seurat v5 layers and v4 slots. Returns a data.frame and (if add_to_meta=TRUE) writes *_QC fields into obj@meta.data (no regression in field names). — calcQCmetrics
code: calcQCmetrics(   obj,   assay = "RNA",   slot = "counts",   species = c("mouse", "human"),   spliced = NULL,   unspliced = NULL,   spliced_obj = NULL,   spliced_assay = NULL,   spliced_layer = "counts",   unspliced_obj = NULL,   unspliced_assay = NULL,   unspliced_layer = "counts",   add_to_meta = TRUE,   debug = getOption("scQCenrich.debug", FALSE) )

title: Calculate QC metrics (UMIs, genes, mito%, MALAT1, stress; optional splicing metrics) Computes nCount/nFeature, pctMT, MALAT1 fraction, stress_score (case-insensitive SYMBOL match), and optional splicing metrics from same object layers or external Seurat objects. Compatible with Seurat v5 layers and v4 slots. Returns a data.frame and (if add_to_meta=TRUE) writes *_QC fields into obj@meta.data (no regression in field names). — calcQCmetrics
text: obj Seurat object assay assay name (default "RNA") slot v4 compatibility ("counts"); ignored layer used internally species "mouse" "human" spliced, unspliced optional layer spec within object; either single layer string (e.g., "spliced") "assay:layer" (e.g., "spliced:counts"). Ignored external objects provided. spliced_obj, unspliced_obj optional external Seurat objects spliced_assay, unspliced_assay assay names external objects spliced_layer, unspliced_layer layer names external objects (e.g., "counts") add_to_meta write *_QC back obj@meta.data debug logical; defaults options(scQCenrich.debug, FALSE)

title: Flag low-quality cells — flagLowQuality
text: Flag low-quality cells

title: Flag low-quality cells — flagLowQuality
code: flagLowQuality(   metrics,   method = c("threshold", "gmm", "hdbscan"),   score_cutoff = 2,   auto_k = 3,   obj = NULL,   celltype_col = NULL,   sample_col = NULL,   rescue_mode = c("moderate", "lenient", "strict", "none"),   cancer_bypass = FALSE,   min_cluster_size = NULL,   doublet_action = c("remove", "borderline", "none"),   doublet_col = "is_doublet",   qc_strength = c("auto", "default", "lenient", "strict"),   save_dir = NULL )

title: Flag low-quality cells — flagLowQuality
text: metrics data.frame QC metrics (rows=cells). contains logical column 'is_doublet' (added calcQCmetrics), honored. method "threshold", "gmm", "hdbscan" score_cutoff integer threshold mode (default 2) auto_k kept API compat (unused ) obj optional Seurat object rescue step celltype_col optional meta column rescue label coherence sample_col optional sample column (passed detector; may NULL) rescue_mode "moderate","lenient","strict", "none" cancer_bypass Logical. TRUE, clusters healthy splicing profiles high removal rates exempt removal-fraction penalty, allowing potential cancer cell populations rescued. Default FALSE. min_cluster_size minimum cluster size consider rescue; NULL = adaptive (~0.5% >=10) doublet_action predicted doublets metrics$is_doublet: "remove" (default), "borderline", "none" doublet_col name doublet flag column metrics (default "is_doublet") qc_strength One c("auto","default","lenient","strict") save_dir Character path NULL. non-NULL, write intermediate CSVs .

title: Flag low-quality cells — flagLowQuality
text: data.frame rownames=cells columns: qc_status, qc_score, cluster, reason,

title: Rescue borderline/removed cells by cluster coherence — rescue_by_coherence
text: obj Seurat object metrics Character vector metric names consider. detected Numeric vector detected gene counts per cell (length = ncol(obj)). celltype_col optional meta column labels (e.g. 'celltype'); missing, label-coherence skipped min_cluster_size minimum cluster size consider rescue (default 25) rescue_mode "moderate" (default), "lenient", "strict", "none" cancer_bypass Logical. TRUE, clusters healthy splicing profiles high removal rates exempt removal-fraction penalty, allowing potential cancer cell populations rescued. Default FALSE. coherence_min Minimum coherence score required rescue (default 0.5). gates_min_pass Minimum number gates cell must pass rescued (default 1).

title: High-level wrapper that computes QC metrics, flags low-quality cells, (optionally) detects doublets and performs lightweight annotation, and renders an HTML report. — run_qc_pipeline
code: run_qc_pipeline(   obj,   species = c("mouse", "human"),   method = c("gmm", "threshold", "auto"),   score_cutoff = 2,   auto_k = 3,   assay = "RNA",   save_dir = "qc_outputs",   report_file = "qc_report.html",   spliced = NULL,   unspliced = NULL,   spliced_obj = NULL,   spliced_assay = NULL,   spliced_layer = "counts",   unspliced_obj = NULL,   unspliced_assay = NULL,   unspliced_layer = "counts",   doublets = c("none", "auto", "doubletfinder", "scdblfinder"),   remove_doublets = TRUE,   sample_col = NULL,   annot_method = c("marker_score", "singleR", "sctype", "none"),   marker_source = c("panglao", "internal", "custom"),   panglao_file = NULL,   canonical_only = TRUE,   ui_max = 0.2,   tissue = NULL,   min_genes = 5,   report_html = TRUE,   theme_bootswatch = "minty",   debug = FALSE,   sctype_db_path = system.file("extdata", "ScTypeDB_full.xlsx", package = "scQCenrich",     mustWork = FALSE),   marker_method = c("findmarkers", "module_score"),   annot_args = NULL,   unknown_min_genes = 2,   unknown_min_margin = 0.01,   unknown_min_top = 0.01,   qc_strength = c("auto", "default", "lenient", "strict"),   rescue_mode = c("moderate", "lenient", "strict", "none"),   cancer_bypass = FALSE,   enrichment_plots = TRUE )

title: High-level wrapper that computes QC metrics, flags low-quality cells, (optionally) detects doublets and performs lightweight annotation, and renders an HTML report. — run_qc_pipeline
text: obj Seurat object species 'mouse' 'human' method 'threshold', 'gmm' 'auto' score_cutoff Integer cutoff used 'threshold' mode auto_k Ignored; kept back-compat assay Assay name save_dir Directory intermediate outputs report_file Output HTML report path spliced, unspliced Back-compat layer strings object spliced_obj, unspliced_obj Optional external Seurat objects spliced_assay, unspliced_assay Assay names external objects spliced_layer, unspliced_layer Layer/slot external objects doublets 'auto','doubletfinder','scdblfinder','none' remove_doublets Logical; TRUE, mark doublets remove/borderline sample_col Optional meta column sample/group annot_method 'SingleR','marker_score','none' marker_source 'panglao', 'internal', 'custom' (used annot_method='marker_score') panglao_file Optional path Panglao TSV (defaults inst/extdata present) canonical_only, ui_max, min_genes Panglao signature filtering knobs tissue Character vector target organs/tissues, e.g. c("Liver", "Kidney"). report_html Logical; TRUE, render report theme_bootswatch Bootswatch theme report debug Logical verbose messages sctype_db_path Path ScType Excel DB. Defaults packaged file marker_method Either "module_score" (default) "findmarkers". annot_args List extra args forwarded auto_annotate(). unknown_min_genes Integer; guard "unknown" assignment. unknown_min_margin Numeric; guard "unknown" assignment. unknown_min_top Integer; guard "unknown" assignment. qc_strength One c("auto","default","lenient","strict"); passed flagLowQuality(). rescue_mode One c("moderate","lenient","strict","none"); controls aggressively borderline cells rescued. Default "moderate". cancer_bypass Logical. TRUE, clusters healthy splicing profiles high removal rates exempt removal-fraction penalty (useful cancer datasets). Default FALSE. enrichment_plots logical. TRUE, run GO/KEGG enrichment plots; FALSE, skip. Default: TRUE.

title: High-level wrapper that computes QC metrics, flags low-quality cells, (optionally) detects doublets and performs lightweight annotation, and renders an HTML report. — run_qc_pipeline
text: list least: $obj (QC-kept), $metrics, $status_df, $report_file. Also returns $obj_all convenience.

what: scQCenrich: State-aware QC for single-cell RNA-seq (with spliced/unspliced support) — scQCenrich-package
title: scQCenrich: State-aware QC for single-cell RNA-seq (with spliced/unspliced support) — scQCenrich-package
text: Features: QC outlier detection (GMM/HDBSCAN/threshold) mito%, genes, stress, MALAT1, (optionally) spliced/unspliced metrics (intronic_frac, unspliced/spliced fractions & ratio). Rescue coherent, non-extreme flagged cells (3-tier keep/borderline/drop). Doublet detection (DoubletFinder / scDblFinder). Automatic cell-type annotation Presentable HTML report (QC×label, removed-cell UMAP, enrichment).

title: scQCenrich: State-aware QC for single-cell RNA-seq (with spliced/unspliced support) — scQCenrich-package
